benefitsPage.title: Your Benefits at a Glance
benefitsPage.description: Explore the complete range of benefits available to Premier America Credit Union employees.

benefitChapters.title: Overview of Available Plans
benefitChapters.description: Overview of all available benefit plans for Premier America Credit Union employees.

children.text: Review and compare all benefit plans available to Premier America Credit Union employees.
Medical Plans

Medical benefits are provided through Anthem Blue Cross with access to Anthem Blue Cross network providers. Coverage includes both in-network and out-of-network providers. All out-of-network services are subject to Reasonable and Customary (R&C) limitations.
1 medical plan available: HDHP
Dental Plans

Dental benefits are provided by Anthem Blue Cross with access to network dentists. Out-of-network provider visits entail higher out-of-pocket costs and possible charges beyond Reasonable and Customary (R&C) amounts. To find a network dentist, visit Anthem Blue Cross website.
1 dental plan available: Anthem Blue Cross - PPO
Vision Plans
Vision benefits are provided through EyeMed with access to EyeMed network providers.
1 vision plan available: INSIGHT - PPO

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for medical coverage.

benefitChapters.title: Eligibility & Qualifying Life Events
benefitChapters.description: Information about who is eligible for benefits, eligible dependents, enrollment, and qualifying life events.

children.text: If you are a full-time team member of Premier America Credit Union who is regularly scheduled to be working at least 20 hours per week on a continual basis (excluding internal and external temporary staff), you are eligible to participate in medical, dental, vision, life and disability plans, and additional benefits.
Eligibility

If you are a full-time team member of Premier America Credit Union who is regularly scheduled to be working at least 20 hours per week on a continual basis (excluding internal and external temporary staff), you are eligible to participate in medical, dental, vision, life and disability plans, and additional benefits.
Full-time team member regularly scheduled at least 20 hours/week on a continual basis
Excluding internal and external temporary staff
Eligible Dependents
Your legal spouse/domestic partner

Children under the age of 26, including birth children, stepchildren, legally adopted children, children placed for adoption, foster children, and children for whom you or your spouse/domestic partner have legal guardianship
Dependent children 26 or more years old, unmarried, primarily supported by you, incapable of self-sustaining employment due to mental or physical disability (periodic certification may be required)
Enrollment
Elections effective first day of the month following hire date
Benefits cannot be changed until the next enrollment period except for Qualifying Life Events (QLE)
Common Qualifying Events
Changes in your life may permit you to update your coverage at points throughout the year, outside of the open enrollment period.
When a Qualifying Life Event (QLE) occurs, you have 30 days to request changes to your coverage. Your change in coverage must be consistent with your change in status.
Change in number of dependents (birth, adoption, loss of eligibility)
Change in legal marital status (marriage, divorce, legal separation)
Change in spouse/domestic partners employment status resulting in loss or gain of coverage
Entitlement to Medicare or Medicaid
Eligibility for coverage through the Marketplace (Healthcare.gov)
Changes in address or location that may affect coverage
Turning 26 and losing coverage through a parent's plan
Change in employment status from full time to part time or vice versa resulting in gain or loss of eligibility
Death in the family leading to change in dependents or loss of coverage
Changes making you no longer eligible for Medicaid or Childrens Health Insurance Program (CHIP)

benefitChapters.title: HDHP Medical Plan
benefitChapters.description: High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) with Anthem Blue Cross. Preventive care covered 100% in-network. Includes prescription drug coverage integrated with medical.

children.text: High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) with Anthem Blue Cross. Preventive care covered 100% in-network. Includes prescription drug coverage integrated with medical.
Copays
Copays are fixed amounts paid at time of service.
E.g., $5 or $15 for medications or office visits.
Deductible
Individual deductible: $1,650 in-network, $4,950 out-of-network.
Family deductible: $4,100 total in-network ($3,300 per family member), $9,900 out-of-network ($4,950 per family member).
Out-of-Pocket Maximum
Individual out-of-pocket max: $4,250 in-network, $12,750 out-of-network.
Family out-of-pocket max: $8,500 total in-network ($4,250 per family member), $25,500 out-of-network ($12,750 per family member).
In-Network vs Out-of-Network
**In-network**
Providers contracted with the insurance company to provide healthcare services at negotiated discounted rates
Visiting in-network providers generally costs less for covered services
**Out-of-network**
Providers not contracted with insurance company; services may cost more or be partially covered
Out-of-network providers may bill you for amounts beyond plan allowances
Visit www.anthem.com or call Customer Care at 833-913-2236 to verify if a provider is in-network.
Help with Selecting Your Medical Coverage
ALEX benefits counselor helps you understand and choose medical coverage options.
Prescription drug coverage through Anthem Blue Cross includes generic, preferred, non-preferred, and specialty drugs.
Virtual visits through LiveHealth Online provide confidential 1-on-1 counseling and virtual medical doctors.
**Toolkit App**: LiveHealth Online app

tables.tableDescription: Bi-weekly associate payroll contributions for HDHP coverage
Comprehensive coverage details for HDHP plan - HSA eligible

benefitChapters.title: Vision Benefits
benefitChapters.description: Vision coverage options and benefits comparison.

children.text: Vision care is provided by EyeMed under the INSIGHT - PPO plan. Coverage includes regular exams, lenses, frames, and contacts with allowances and copays as shown. Services have specific frequency limits.
Notes & Disclaimers
Includes LASIK and PRK benefit; Hearing care; Eyeglass lens upgrades; Continued eyewear savings.

benefitChapters.title: Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
benefitChapters.description: The Team Member Assistance Program (EAP) provides confidential mental, emotional, and physical health support free of charge for team members and their families, regardless of medical plan enrollment.

children.text: The Team Member Assistance Program (EAP) provides confidential mental, emotional, and physical health support free of charge for team members and their families, regardless of medical plan enrollment. It offers 24/7 access to licensed professionals by phone, and a limited number of face-to-face visits per issue. Additional services include legal and financial assistance.
Services available 24/7 via telephone, face-to-face, or web-based assistance.
Overview

The Team Member Assistance Program (EAP) provides confidential mental, emotional, and physical health support free of charge for team members and their families, regardless of medical plan enrollment. It offers 24/7 access to licensed professionals by phone, and a limited number of face-to-face visits per issue. Additional services include legal and financial assistance.
Free Visits
Designated number of face-to-face visits per issue at no cost.
Services include:
Emotional health and wellbeing
Job pressures
Stress, anxiety, depression
Alcohol or drug dependency
Marriage or family problems
Grief and loss
Financial or legal advice

benefitChapters.title: Health Savings Account (HSA) & Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
benefitChapters.description: HSA and FSA account options for tax-advantaged savings.

children.text: Understand your tax-advantaged savings account options.
What is a Health Savings Account?
Your HSA can be used for qualified expenses for you, your legal spouse/domestic partner, and/or tax dependent(s), even if they are not covered by your plan.
If not enrolled in an HDHP but have unused HSA funds from a previous account, those funds can still be used for qualified expenses.
Benefits of HSA
HSA funds never expire; unused funds roll over annually.
You can contribute to your HSA on a pre-tax basis through payroll deduction.
Employer provides seed money of $1,100 for team member or $2,100 for family.
You own your HSA account and manage it.
Account is portable and has no vesting or forfeiture provisions.
Who is eligible for an HSA?
Must be enrolled in an HSA-eligible High Deductible Health Plan.
Cannot be covered by non-HDHP spouse/domestic partner or parents plan.
Cannot have a Healthcare FSA or Health Reimbursement Account.
Cannot be claimed as a dependent on anothers tax return.
Cannot be enrolled in Medicare or TRICARE.
Cannot have received VA medical benefits except for service-related care in past 90 days.
How to manage your HSA account
Use debit card for qualified medical expenses; must have funds available.
Keep receipts for tax purposes.
Can save and roll funds over year to year.
Eligible expenses
Doctors visits, eye exams, prescription expenses, laser eye surgery, menstrual products, PPE, over-the-counter medications.
What is a Flexible Spending Account?

Flexible Spending Account (FSA) is a tax-free account you contribute pre-tax dollars to pay for certain out-of-pocket medical, dental, vision, and dependent care expenses. Limited to $3,300 annually for Healthcare FSA and up to $5,000 for Dependent Care FSA. You cannot have both HSA and Healthcare FSA simultaneously. Includes Healthcare FSA, Dependent Care FSA, and Limited Use FSA (for dental and vision).
Understanding different types of FSAs
Healthcare FSA cannot be held with an HSA.
Dependent Care FSA pays for child or elder care expenses.
Limited Use FSA is for dental and vision expenses for HDHP and HSA holders.

benefitChapters.title: Survivor Benefits
benefitChapters.description: Basic Life and AD&D insurance is provided automatically to full-time team members through Mutual of Omaha, providing $50,000 in coverage. Coverage includes Basic Life and Accidental Death and Dismembe

children.text: Basic Life and AD&D insurance is provided automatically to full-time team members through Mutual of Omaha, providing $50,000 in coverage. Coverage includes Basic Life and Accidental Death and Dismemberment (AD&D).
RS&H provides Basic Life and Accidental Death & Dismemberment Insurance (AD&D)

Basic Life and AD&D insurance is provided automatically to full-time team members through Mutual of Omaha, providing $50,000 in coverage. Coverage includes Basic Life and Accidental Death and Dismemberment (AD&D).
Coverage amount
Basic Life and AD&D insurance benefit is $50,000.
Eligible team members may purchase voluntary life insurance in increments up to $500,000 (not exceeding 5 times annual salary).
Spouse coverage available up to 100% of team members election up to $250,000.
Children coverage available up to 100% of team members election up to $10,000 ($2,000 minimum).
AD&D Coverage

Premier America Credit Union provides Basic Life and AD&D insurance coverage through Mutual of Omaha. Coverage guarantees benefit payments continue to designated survivors in case of death or dismemberment.
Basic Life and AD&D insurance benefit is $50,000.
Coverage is automatic for full-time team members even if other coverage is waived.
Other Voluntary benefits

Optional voluntary life insurance plans are available for team members, spouses/domestic partners, and children with premiums paid through payroll deductions. Evidence of insurability is required for higher coverage amounts.
Voluntary life insurance
Voluntary AD&D insurance
Designating Beneficiaries
Name a primary and contingent beneficiary with full information. Benefits payments cannot usually be made to a minor; proceeds may be held until minor reaches age 18.

benefitChapters.title: Supplemental Health
benefitChapters.description: Supplemental health benefits provided by Allstate.

children.text: Provider: Allstate
What is Supplemental Health?
Supplemental health benefits provide additional coverage that helps cover expenses not paid by medical plans.
Coverage options include accident insurance, critical illness insurance, and hospital indemnity insurance.
Critical Illness Coverage
Guaranteed issue coverage with no medical questions.
Benefits payable based on date of covered event or diagnosis.
Wellness benefit payable once per calendar year for specified services.
Covered illness include:
Cancer
Heart Attack
Stroke
Coma
Paralysis
Loss of Sight
Loss of Hearing
Loss of Speech
Benign Brain Tumor
Coronary Disease
Carcinoma in Situ
Major Organ Failure
Skin Cancer
Life Threatening Cancer
Payment frequency
Payment made as lump sum upon diagnosis or event.
Accident Coverage
Benefits payable for accidents occurring outside of work.
Helps pay deductibles, copays, and day-to-day expenses.
Benefits paid to you directly to use as you wish.
Notes & Disclaimers
This is a fixed indemnity policy, not health insurance.
Payments under this policy are limited dollar amounts and not based on medical bills.
Policy is not a substitute for comprehensive health insurance.

benefitChapters.title: Income Protection (Disability)
benefitChapters.description: Short-term and long-term disability insurance and income protection benefits.

children.text: Premier America Credit Union offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance to help protect your income in case of injury or illness.
Understand your disability insurance options.
Overview
Premier America Credit Union offers both Short-Term Disability (STD) and Long-Term Disability (LTD) insurance to help protect your income in case of injury or illness.
STD replaces 55% of income for up to 90 days after a short waiting period.
LTD replaces 50% of income for long-term disability, benefits last until Social Security Normal Retirement Age or recovery.
Short-Term Disability (STD) Insurance
Replaces 55% of your income for short-term disability.
Weekly maximum benefit is $1,075.
Elimination period: Day 1 for accident/injury; Day 8 for illness.
Maximum benefit period: 90 days.
Basic Long-Term Disability (LTD) Insurance
Available at no cost to team members.
Replaces 50% of your income for long-term disability.
Monthly maximum benefit: $6,000.
Elimination period: 90 days.
Maximum benefit period: until Social Security Normal Retirement Age or recovery.
Notes & Disclaimers
If employer pays LTD premiums, benefits are taxable income when paid.
If team member pays with after-tax dollars, benefits are not taxable.

benefitChapters.title: Additional Voluntary Benefits
benefitChapters.description: Premier America Credit Union offers various voluntary benefits for team members and their families to make life easier and provide financial protection.

children.text: Premier America Credit Union offers various voluntary benefits for team members and their families to make life easier and provide financial protection.

items.title: Pet Insurance

children.text: Pet insurance provides coverage for veterinary expenses related to accidents and illnesses, including X-rays, medications, vet visits, surgeries, and hospital stays.

**How it works**
Policies available for dogs, cats, birds, reptiles, and exotic pets. Optional wellness coverage available for dogs and cats.
Provider: Nationwide
Accident and illness coverage
Preventive care reimbursement for wellness plans

items.link: Contact Nationwide to enroll or for more information.

children.text: The Elite Identity plans include data, device, and identity protection, along with financial wellness solutions.

**How it works**
Includes proactive privacy tools and identity theft insurance. Provides professional restoration services and personal finance tools.
Provider: Experian
The 529 plan helps families save for future education expenses, similar to IRAs and 401(k) plans for retirement.

**How it works**
Administered by ScholarShare529; handles paperwork and oversees investments.
